text: Chapter 14 teaches arpeggio patterns over minor seventh chords through directed pattern direction reversal—practicing chord tones in both descending and ascending directions to internalize the structure. The chapter introduces an explicit chord duration rule (each chord lasts two beats) that governs the metrical framework for all exercises. Key topic labels include pattern direction reversal and chord duration rule, which together form the characteristic practice methodology applied throughout. The work emphasizes reversing directional arpeggio patterns as the mechanism for mastering the Dorian sound, making directionality the defining technical move of this chapter.
